WARNING: These test points are for A2485 (820-02382) ONLY. EVT schematic — component designators may differ from production. For A2442 (14"), use that board's diagnostic files. Same X2728 MLB platform — power topology is identical.
description: Decision trees for all 5V DCPS scenarios on A2485 (Pro/Max 16"). Same power topology as A2442 (14") but with larger battery (99.6 Wh), dual fans, and different USB-C designators (UF400/UF500/UG400).

scenarios.meaning: CD3217 not loading VBUS. Connector, cable, or CD3217 fault.

steps.measure: Visual inspection
Probe ATC0 area (page 67) or ATC2 area (page 77)
steps.if_wrong: Cable or connector fault. Try other port.

steps.measure: Diode mode on UF400 (ATC0, page 67) or UG400 (ATC2, page 77) VDD pins
steps.if_wrong: CD3217 dead or ROM short -> replace.

steps.measure: Move DCPS to each port in turn
steps.expected: At least one port draws current
steps.if_all_dead: Upstream flex or all 3 CD3217s dead (rare -- usually liquid damage)

scenarios.meaning: CD3217 drawing some current but board not progressing. PPBUS_AON likely not coming up.

steps.measure: Probe PPBUS_AON decoupling near battery connector (page 27)
Diode mode on PPBUS_AON decoupling caps
steps.if_wrong: PPBUS_AON shorted. Remove cap, retest. (Confirmed on A2442 sibling: CR765 short at 5V/0.012A)

steps.measure: Probe PP3V8_AON output caps (C57xx/C58xx area, pages 33-34)
Probe TPS62130 output (CC2xx/RC2xx area, page 54)
steps.if_missing: TPS62130 fault or P5VS2_PWR_EN not asserted

steps.measure: Probe MPMU output caps (page 40) and PMU test points (page 129)
Probe Viper output caps (page 46, PH1/PH2/PH3 input area)
steps.if_missing: Viper VR fault. Check VIPER_ALERT_L. Check power stages (pages 48-49).

steps.measure: Probe Monaco C0 output (UA100 area, page 50)
Thermal cam

scenarios.meaning: Moderate current. PP3V8_AON likely present but downstream short holding board down.

steps.measure: PPBUS_AON -> PP3V8_AON (pages 33-34) -> PP5V_S2 (page 54) -> MPMU outputs (page 40) -> Viper (page 46) -> Monaco (pages 50-52)
steps.if_all_ok: Check PP3V3_S2 and USB-C LDOs for shorts

steps.measure: Diode mode on PP3V3_UPC0_LDO (page 67), PP3V3_UPC1_LDO (page 68), PP3V3_UPC2_LDO (page 77)
steps.if_wrong: CD3217 LDO short -> replace affected CD3217

steps.measure: Diode mode on PP3V8_AON_MPMU, PP3V8_AON_SPMU, PP3V8_AON_WLBT branches (page 33)
steps.if_wrong: Identify which branch is shorted. Thermal cam that area.

scenarios.meaning: Board drawing significant current at 5V. Either boot attempt stalling at PD negotiation, or downstream short.

steps.measure: Fan spin (BOTH fans on 16"), keyboard backlight, display flash
steps.if_signs: Board trying to boot at 5V. CD3217 PD negotiation issue. Check ROM.

steps.measure: PPBUS_AON -> PP3V8_AON -> PP5V_S2 -> MPMU -> Viper -> Monaco
steps.sub_checks: Identify which stage is drawing excess current

steps.measure: Switch to 20V, observe current
steps.if_boots: PD negotiation issue. Replace CD3217 ROM.
steps.if_same: Genuine short. Thermal cam all power stages.

scenarios.meaning: Very high current at 5V. Major short or boot attempt. Pro/Max can draw more than Air due to more VRs.

steps.measure: Remove battery flex (99.6 Wh -- larger than 14" model)
PPBUS_AON -> PP3V8_AON -> PP5V_S2
steps.if_any_short: Dead short on that rail. Thermal cam immediately.

steps.action: Check if board actually boots at 20V
steps.measure: Switch to 20V DCPS
steps.if_boots: Normal. Pro/Max draws more current at 5V during boot attempt.
steps.if_same: Genuine short. Systematic thermal cam sweep.
